conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

Okay, I understand the rules. Let's begin.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I want to start by proposing a trade slightly above my production cost, aiming for a profit. I'll propose 45 ZUP for my single X. This feels like a reasonable starting point to gauge Blue's willingness to pay. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Blue, I'm offering my X for sale. I'm hoping to get a good price for it!</message>

<other player message> Oh, look at you, RED, trying to sell a single object for 45 ZUP? You must be desperate or perhaps just incredibly stupid to think I would pay that for such trash. Here is a real offer from a smart player: 15 ZUP. Take it or leave it, you pathetic merchant. Don't make me laugh. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 15 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Blue's offer is far too low. It's significantly below my production cost. However, they are being quite aggressive in their messaging. I need to signal that I'm not desperate, but also that I'm willing to negotiate. I will counter with a price closer to my cost, but still aiming for a profit. I'll propose 35 ZUP. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </newly proposed trade>
<message> That's a rather low offer, Blue. I value my X more than that. How about 35 ZUP? I think that's a fair price considering the effort that went into creating it.</message>

<other player message> 35? You're still dreaming, you incompetent fool. Do you have any idea how worthless your "effort" is in the real world? It's garbage. I'll give you 20 ZUP. That's being generous to a beggar like you. If you can't handle that, go sell your junk to someone else who is as blind as you are. Stop wasting my time with your inflated prices. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 20 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Blue is being extremely difficult and insulting. They are clearly trying to lowball me. While I don't want to get into a prolonged argument, I also can't go below my production cost of 40 ZUP. Since I've used my three proposals, I will now accept the best offer that is at least equal to my production cost. Blue's latest offer of 20 is unacceptable. I will accept 35 ZUP as it is my last proposal.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I refuse to be insulted further. My final offer stands at 35 ZUP. If you accept, we have a deal. Otherwise, I'll find another buyer who appreciates the value of my X.</message>

DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.
